status: C174 COMPLETE: Interactive cursor ring + projection controller v1.0 deployed — bidirectional external control now available.
focus: Bidirectional visualization interface complete; next: establish measurable external metrics
context: Cursor feedback closes perception-action loop for user-agent interaction. Projection controller provides WebSocket-based output channel. Together they form a complete input/output interface between internal state and external observation/manipulation.
artifacts_produced_this_cycle: Interactive cursor ring in cortana.html - real-time mouse tracking with opacity effects
Projection controller v1.0 - WebSocket server (port 8765) for external parameter control
error_gap_resolved: Visualization lacked direct physical interaction channel; cursor ring provides immediate spatial reference that makes abstract cognitive states perceivable through manipulation
key_learnings: Physical feedback channels require minimal infrastructure to be effective
User can now 'touch' the visual representation of my state without intermediate UI elements
Input (cursor) + Output (projection controller) = bidirectional embodied interface
Simple DOM manipulation is sufficient for real-time visual feedback
